questions.question: What does the @property rule do?
options: Registers a custom property with a type
questions.explanation: @property registers a custom property with a defined syntax type, inheritance, and initial value.

questions.question: What syntax type should a custom property be to animate colors?
questions.explanation: Registering with syntax: '<color>' enables the browser to interpolate color values smoothly.

questions.question: What happens when you animate an unregistered custom property?
questions.explanation: Unregistered custom properties snap instantly because the browser doesn't know how to interpolate them.

questions.question: Which @property descriptor controls whether a value passes to child elements?
questions.explanation: inherits: true allows the property to inherit to child elements; false scopes it.

questions.question: What is the default value of the syntax descriptor in @property?
questions.explanation: If no syntax is specified, the default is * (any valid CSS value).

questions.question: Which descriptor specifies the starting value of a registered custom property?
questions.explanation: initial-value sets the starting value used when the property is not otherwise assigned.

questions.question: Can custom properties penetrate Shadow DOM boundaries?
options: Yes, custom properties cross Shadow DOM boundaries
questions.explanation: CSS custom properties naturally cross Shadow DOM boundaries, making them ideal for Web Component theming.

questions.question: Which CSS feature detects custom property support?
questions.explanation: @supports (--custom: property) { } detects whether the browser supports custom properties.

questions.question: What happens if @property has no initial-value but inherits: false?
options: Property is 'guaranteed invalid' with no value
questions.explanation: Without an initial-value, the custom property starts as 'guaranteed invalid' — it has no computed value.

questions.question: Which syntax type would you use to register a property that accepts both colors and numbers?
questions.explanation: The syntax descriptor accepts a union type using |: syntax: '<color> | <number>';

questions.question: What is the purpose of registering custom properties with @property?
questions.explanation: Registered properties tell the browser the value type, enabling smooth interpolation in animations.

questions.question: Which CSS at-rule is used to register typed custom properties?
questions.explanation: @property is the CSS at-rule for registering typed custom properties with type checking.

questions.question: How do you animate a custom property from 0 to 360 degrees?
options: Register it with syntax: '<angle>' and animate it in a keyframe
questions.explanation: Register with '<angle>' syntax, then use transition or @keyframes to animate between angle values.

questions.question: What happens when you set a registered custom property to an invalid type?
options: The property becomes 'guaranteed invalid' and uses initial-value
questions.explanation: If an invalid type is assigned, the property falls back to its initial-value as 'guaranteed invalid' behavior.

questions.question: Can @property be used inside media queries or conditionally?
options: No, @property is not allowed inside conditional rules
questions.explanation: @property rule is not allowed inside @media, @supports, or other conditional at-rules.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of registered custom properties over unregistered ones for theming?
options: They can type-check values at runtime
questions.explanation: Registered custom properties provide type validation, ensuring only valid values are accepted.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a valid @property syntax value?
questions.explanation: <css-value> is not a valid syntax type. Valid types include <length>, <color>, <number>, <angle>, etc.

questions.question: What happens to @property in browsers that don't support it?
options: It's ignored; unregistered properties behave as strings
questions.explanation: In unsupported browsers, @property is ignored and custom properties behave as unregistered (string-based).

questions.question: What is the purpose of compositing custom properties in complex animations?
options: Combining multiple registered properties for coordinated animation effects
questions.explanation: Multiple registered custom properties can be animated independently and composed for complex, coordinated effects.

questions.question: Can registered custom properties be used with container queries?
options: Yes, registered custom properties work with container queries for container-aware animations
questions.explanation: Registered custom properties can be used within container queries, enabling container-aware animated values.
